title: Per-project dashboard still uses item-level batch progress after I-00036

steps.description: Backend: create dashboard/utils/batch_progress.py with compute_batch_step_progress(project_id, batch_ids, db) -> dict[batch_id, pct] using one aggregated SQL query joining WorkflowStep on BatchItem; done = completed + skipped only; total = all WorkflowStep rows for items in each batch; returns 0 for batches with zero steps or not in DB

Code review: S01 (SQL correctness, project_id scoping on BOTH BatchItem and WorkflowStep joins, done set = {completed, skipped} only, no N+1, dict covers all requested batch_ids — missing → 0, type signature, helper placement under dashboard/utils/ per project convention, no tests file expected here — tests belong to S05)

Frontend: wire both dashboard routers to the shared helper. project_dashboard.py:_active_batches() — replace line 137 pct calc with helper call (keep existing BatchItem aggregation for completed_items/total_items intact). batches.py:_all_batches() — replace the inline Python-side WorkflowStep loop (lines 213-226) with helper call. Do NOT edit templates. Do NOT change BatchSummary or BatchRow dataclass shapes.

Code review: S03 (both routers call the helper, completed_items/total_items item-based preserved in both, no template edits, no dataclass shape changes, imports tidy, existing query-optimisation patterns respected, no business logic accidentally moved out of helper into router)

Tests: reproduction test (1 item, 10 steps, 3 done → dashboard and batches both 30, parity assert) + regression set (empty batch, zero steps, all-done, skipped counted, failed/needs_fix/in_progress NOT counted, multi-batch aggregation via helper directly, project_id scoping, missing batch_id → 0) + HTTP smoke test (TestClient hits both routes, identical percentage substring). Every assertion pins a specific expected value (I003 lesson). Parity lock: assert dash.progress_pct == full.progress_pct on the same seeded batch.

Code review: S05 (parity assertion present, semantic correctness of percentages, coverage matches design-doc regression matrix, reproduction test would fail on pre-fix code, testcontainer compliance, no DB mocking, no shape-only assertions)

Final cross-layer review: AC1-AC5 completeness, single source of truth for progress_pct (both routers call helper), completed_items/total_items still item-level per user instruction, templates untouched, parity test in place, no scope creep

QV: Browser verification — seed partial-progress batch via e2e_fixtures, then verify IDENTICAL non-zero progress percentage on BOTH /project/{id}/ (Active Batches card) AND /project/{id}/batches (Progress column). Assert the two views agree. Capture post-fix screenshots matching the pre-fix pair.
